diff:
diff --git a/doc/yaml_docs.go b/doc/yaml_docs.go
index 20950fe..4328adc 100644
--- a/doc/yaml_docs.go
+++ b/doc/yaml_docs.go
@@ -42,6 +42,7 @@ type cmdDoc struct {
        Options          []cmdOption `yaml:",omitempty"`
        InheritedOptions []cmdOption `yaml:"inherited_options,omitempty"`
        Example          string      `yaml:",omitempty"`
+       Aliases          []string    `yaml:",omitempty"`
        SeeAlso          []string    `yaml:"see_also,omitempty"`
 }
 
@@ -108,6 +109,10 @@ func GenYamlCustom(cmd *cobra.Command, w io.Writer, linkHandler func(string) str
                yamlDoc.Example = cmd.Example
        }
 
+       if len(cmd.Aliases) > 0 {
+               yamlDoc.Aliases = cmd.Aliases
+       }
+
        flags := cmd.NonInheritedFlags()
        if flags.HasFlags() {
                yamlDoc.Options = genFlagResult(flags)

key_decisions.reasoning: Aliases is a string slice on Command; keeping the type as []string preserves proper YAML list serialization

solve_notes: Added an `Aliases []string` field to the `cmdDoc` struct in `doc/yaml_docs.go` with `yaml:",omitempty"` tag, then populated it from `cmd.Aliases` in `GenYamlCustom` when the slice is non-empty. No other files needed changing.
task_text:
Fix yaml_docs.go not including the Aliases field

The YAML documentation generator in `doc/yaml_docs.go` builds a `cmdDoc`
struct from the `cobra.Command` and marshals it to YAML. However, it never
populates the `Aliases` field — commands that have aliases (e.g.
`cmd.Aliases = ["mv", "move"]`) produce YAML output that silently omits
the `aliases` key. Fix `GenYamlCustom` to include the `aliases` field in
the YAML output when a command has aliases defined.

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: Calls GenYaml which delegates to GenYamlCustom, exercising the full struct-population path including the new Aliases block
Also calls GenYaml, exercises the same population path

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum_sufficient set is the edited struct and function. No additional context is needed — the struct fields are all simple and the population pattern is identical to the existing Example/Usage blocks.
